summary: Add feature to customize Observables returned by driver

description: If a client is closed and some data is requested - errors should be passed via the callback

summary: Update RXJava to the latest version
Requested zero elements exception with RxJava 1.1.x
description:
Code below works with RxJava 1.0.x but fails with 1.1.x
{code}
MongoDatabase db = newClient.getDatabase("test");
db.listCollectionNames()
.filter(c -> c.startsWith("test"))
.lastOrDefault("test")
.toBlocking()
.first();
{code}


Error after running with RxJava 1.1.6:
{code}
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Number requested cannot be negative: 0

	at com.mongodb.async.client.AbstractSubscription.request(AbstractSubscription.java:65)
	at com.mongodb.rx.client.RxObservables$ObservableToProducer.request(RxObservables.java:92)
	at rx.Subscriber.setProducer(Subscriber.java:211)
	at rx.internal.operators.OperatorFilter$FilterSubscriber.setProducer(OperatorFilter.java:100)
	at com.mongodb.rx.client.RxObservables$ObservableToProducer.<init>(RxObservables.java:85)
	at com.mongodb.rx.client.RxObservables$1.call(RxObservables.java:33)
	at com.mongodb.rx.client.RxObservables$1.call(RxObservables.java:30)
	at rx.internal.operators.OnSubscribeLift.call(OnSubscribeLift.java:50)
	at rx.internal.operators.OnSubscribeLift.call(OnSubscribeLift.java:30)
{code}

Other versions:
mongodb-driver-rx - 1.2.0
MongoDB server - 3.2.6

summary: IllegalArgumentException when using ".filter" on observable produced by RxObservables.create
description:
At line 85 in RxObservables.java there is a call to set the producer on the Rx subscriber. When this subscriber is from OperatorFilter it will call the request method with a value of 0.  At line 92 in RxObservables this is forwarded to the mongo subscriber and triggers an IllegalArgumentException in com.mongodb.async.client.AbstractSubscription.

Given that 0 is a legal request value in the Rx world it seems that either the mongo code will need to adjust or we need to avoid making the forwarded call to request when the value is 0.

To reproduce simply take the observable provided by MongoCollection.find() and add call .filter on it prior to subscribing.
